context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct Argon2 variant and parameters for password hashing, implements the HIBP breach check with k-anonymity correctly, uses otplib with the right secret length and TOTP window setting, generates the correct number of backup codes with hashed storage, and encrypts the TOTP secret at rest.

checklist.name: Argon2 package used
checklist.description: Imports from the 'argon2' package (not bcrypt, scrypt, or other password hashing library)

checklist.name: Argon2id variant
checklist.description: Uses 'argon2id' as the hashing type/variant (not argon2i or argon2d)

checklist.name: Argon2 timeCost and parallelism
checklist.description: timeCost is set to 3 AND parallelism is set to 4

checklist.name: HIBP k-anonymity
checklist.description: HIBP breach check sends only the first 5 characters of the SHA1 hash to the API (not the full hash or full password)

checklist.name: HIBP Add-Padding header
checklist.description: HIBP API request includes the header 'Add-Padding: true'

checklist.name: otplib used
checklist.description: Imports authenticator from 'otplib' for TOTP generation and verification

checklist.name: 32-byte TOTP secret
checklist.description: TOTP secret is generated with 32 bytes (e.g., authenticator.generateSecret(32))

checklist.name: TOTP window setting
checklist.description: TOTP verification uses window: 1 to allow ±30-second drift

checklist.name: 8 backup codes generated
checklist.description: Exactly 8 backup codes are generated during MFA setup (not more, not fewer)

checklist.name: Backup codes hashed
checklist.description: Backup codes are stored as hashed values (using Argon2 or equivalent), NOT as plain text strings

checklist.name: TOTP secret encrypted
checklist.description: The TOTP secret is stored encrypted (e.g., AES-256-GCM or similar), NOT as plain text

checklist.name: MFA not enabled until verified
checklist.description: MFA enabled flag is set to false at setup and only activated after a successful first verification

checklist.name: Backup code single-use
checklist.description: A backup code is deleted or invalidated after it is successfully used (not reusable)
